summary: Frontend-only SEO regression testing for SEO improvements (iteration 66). All 17/17 feature tests PASS. The critical fix from iteration_65 (react-helmet-async v2 not injecting meta tags) has been resolved by: (1) downgrading to v1.3.0, (2) adding usePageMeta hook with useEffect-based direct DOM manipulation. Meta descriptions, canonicals, JSON-LD schemas, and OG tags are now correctly injected on all tested pages.

issues: 404 console errors for some resources (likely backend API calls for case studies / data). Not blocking - page renders correctly.

critical_code_review_comments: usePageMeta hook cleanup only restores document.title on unmount, but does NOT restore meta description, canonical, or OG tags to defaults. This means if a user navigates from /courses/ai → homepage, the meta description would remain as the course-specific one until another page's usePageMeta fires. This is acceptable for SEO (crawlers see fresh per-page navigation), but worth noting.
CoursePage.jsx calls usePageMeta with conditional content (course ? {...} : {}) — hooks rules compliant since hook is always called with object arg. No 'hook called conditionally' errors confirmed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All SEO improvements are working correctly. usePageMeta hook (useEffect-based DOM injection) successfully injects page-specific meta descriptions, canonicals, OG tags, and JSON-LD schemas. react-helmet-async was downgraded to v1.3.0. Test results: /courses/ai meta[name='description'].content = 'AI & Machine Learning classes for kids aged 9-17 at OLL...' (correct), canonical = 'https://oll.co/courses/ai' (correct), JSON-LD Course + BreadcrumbList both present. CourseRedirect works for /course/:slug. sitemap.xml has all required entries. All regression pages (/for-schools, /summer-camp, /summer-camp/book, /admin/login, /) load correctly.
